events.title: Morning Glow: Garden Yoga Sundays with Jené
events.description: @ Awaken your senses and cultivate clarity for the week ahead with an invigorating morning practice nestled in the beauty of Zilker Botanical Garden. This hour-long session gently wakes up the body, builds vital energy, and restores mental focus through restorative movement. events.title: Home Grown Austin Show & Sale – Day 2
events.description: @ Come experience The Garden Club of Austin’s 67th Annual Plant & Flower Show and Sale. You’ll find lots of plants and flowers perfect for Austin, and the extra added feature of the Austin Area Begonia Society selling plants both days. events.title: National Garden Week Kickoff Expo
events.description: @ As part of the event’s celebration of the Garden’s history, community partnerships, and educational programs, Austin Area Garden Council will share highlights from its 70-year history during guided Garden tours and provide information about its archive collection being donated to the Austin History Center. events.title: First Annual Garden Book and Puzzle Sale – Day 2
events.description: @ Come to our sale and help support the garden we celebrate this week, Zilker Botanical Garden, and the organizations that support the garden, Austin Area Garden Council and Zilker Botanical Garden Conservancy. events.title: Chess Academy: Open Play
events.description: The Round Rock Public Library Chess Academy meets on Sunday afternoons from 1:30 to 4:30 p.m. Our aim is to make this incredible game more accessible for everyone. events.title: Genealogy Coach
events.description: Need advice on where to find that elusive record or data? Visit the Reference Desk, Third Floor, from 2 to 4 p.m., on Sundays, for a one-on-one session with Genealogy Coach Mandy Cerrillo. Family history researchers now have the opportunity to receive assistance, one-on-one, from someone with years of experience. To reserve your one-hour slot, Register Here

events.title: Youth Open Chess Play
events.description: Are you looking to play chess with other youth? Stop by the second-floor Youth Services Program Space and build your chess skills by playing with others. Some chess sets will be provided. Feel free to bring your own chess set. Recommended for ages 6 and up with a caregiver. This is a come-and-go event.

events.venue: Event Space at Cedar Park Public Library

events.title: Family Construction Zone
events.description: Head over to the Round Rock Public Library for some collaborative construction as we bring out the Library’s large collections of LEGO® bricks, Keva Planks, magnetic building sets, and blocks for the entire family to enjoy. Join the come-and-go fun in the library's Youth Program Space, 2nd Floor . Recommended for ages 5+.

events.title: DIY Flower Imprinting
events.description: Join us in welcoming summer through this fun craft. Learn a new, fun way to preserve the beautiful blooms of nature.

events.title: BK Bones: Dinosaurs of Texas (ages 5+)
events.description: Meet Paleontologist BK Bones ! She will share a peek back in time into prehistoric Texas, where fascinating dinosaurs and other amazing creatures roamed the world. events.title: BK Bones - Jurassic Park vs. Real Science (ages 11-18)
events.description: Meet paleontologists BK Bones for this exploration! In 1993, the movie Jurassic Park was released and became a cultural phenomenon. Older and younger siblings are welcome. The regular Toddlertime schedule is: 9:30 a.m. Tuesdays 9:30 a.m. Wednesdays 10:30 a.m. Wednesdays Join us in the first-floor meeting room at Round Rock Publi

events.title: Preschool Storytime
events.description: Preschool Storytime is a mix of stories, songs, playful activities, and creative movement. This program is best enjoyed by children ages 3 – 6, alongside their caregivers. Older and younger siblings are welcome. Join us in the 2nd floor Youth Program Space at Round Rock Public Library .

events.title: Babytime (0 -18 months)
events.description: Babies and their caregivers will enjoy this storytime activity featuring books, songs, rhymes, and playful activities–plus some time to socialize. We’ll gather in the Youth Services Program Space (2nd floor). Available times for ages 0 - 18 months are: 9:30 a.m. and 2:00 p.m. on Wednesdays and Thursdays

events.title: Evening Serenity: Weekend Wind-Down with Jené
events.description: @ Transition seamlessly from the busy work week into a restful weekend with a soothing evening practice at Zilker Botanical Garden. Held indoors within the peaceful, historic Greene Room, this hour-long session helps you de-stress, release tension, and reset your mind. The practice blends active yet gentle movements to untangle the week’s stress, pairing min

events.title: Library on the Go at Frontier Park
events.description: Our pop-up library service, Library on the Go , is intended to reach people in our community who may not have easy access to the library over the summer. We hope to encourage reading and literacy, connect with the community, and provide library services in a fun and engaging way. Library on the Go will be at Frontier Park and Meadow Lake Park once a month al

events.title: Bee-O-Diversity: Meet Austin’s Native Bees
events.description: @ Presented by Austin Parks & Recreation’s Community Gardens Program Explore the amazing world of native bees! Learn about local species, what they need, and where to find them in the Zilker Botanical Garden. Register Here! Photo courtesy of Deana Dossey, taken at Gus Garcia Community Garden. Species: Pruinose Squash Bee ( Xenoglossa pruinosa ). Meet your Pr

events.title: Pokémon Club
events.description: Pokémon fans, drop in for come-and-go fun with fellow Pokémon fans. Play, trade, chat, draw–you choose! Engagement Zones include: • Arts & Crafts Corner for creative expression • Trade Tables monitored by adult volunteers • Casual TCG Battles (Expanded format), VGC, and PGO/Pocket play • Learn to Play sessions for new

events.title: Concert with Lucas Miller, the Singing Zoologist
events.description: Bring your curious kids and your best dance moves to today's very special Family Storytime. We're having a concert with Lucas Miller, The Singing Zoologist ! This concert features bopping tunes packed with bite-sized information about amazing animals. Enjoy a sneak preview! Families with children of all ages are welcome. Children ages 18 months – 8 yea

events.title: Unearthing Texas Stories: A TxDOT Archaeologist Explains How
events.description: Before building roads, Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT) archeologists review how transportation projects might affect Texas' historical and cultural environment. events.title: Chess Academy: Open Play
events.description: The Round Rock Public Library Chess Academy meets on Sunday afternoons from 1:30 to 4:30 p.m. Our aim is to make this incredible game more accessible for everyone. Beginner and intermediate-level chess players can join us for our weekly sessions to learn new strategies and play against each other. Our chess coaches, Mr. Wassim Nassif and Mr. Carmen Chairez,

events.title: Family Movie - Zootopia 2
events.description: Bring some snacks, pillows, and blankets, and watch a family-friendly film in the library’s second-floor Youth Program Space. Today’s film is Zootopia 2 ©Walt Disney Pictures (Rated PG) (Runtime 108 minutes).. Thanks to the Friends of the Round Rock Public Library for purchasing the movie license that allows us to share this film with you.

events.title: Genealogy Coach
events.description: Need advice on where to find that elusive record or data? Visit the Reference Desk, Third Floor, from 2 to 4 p.m., on Sundays, for a one-on-one session with Genealogy Coach Mandy Cerrillo. Family history researchers now have the opportunity to receive assistance, one-on-one, from someone with years of experience. To reserve your one-hour slot, Register Here

events.title: Family Game Day
events.description: It’s gaming day at the Round Rock Public Library . Come play board games pulled from the library’s extensive collection. Enjoy come-and-go fun in the Youth Program Space, 2nd Floor .

events.title: Teen Gavel Club
events.description: The Teen Gavel Club is dedicated to teaching Middle School and High School students public speaking, storytelling and presentation and leadership skills in a supportive, fun environment. events.title: Austin Symphony Orchestra July 4th Concert and Fireworks presented by H-E-B
events.description: Austin's largest Independence Day celebration: patriotic music by the Austin Symphony Orchestra at Vic Mathias (Auditorium) Shores with a fireworks finale over Lady Bird Lake. Gates open 4:30PM; the majority of the grounds is free and open to the public.

events.title: Youth Open Chess Play
events.description: Are you looking to play chess with other youth? Stop by the second-floor Youth Services Program Space and build your chess skills by playing with others. Some chess sets will be provided. Feel free to bring your own chess set. Recommended for ages 6 and up with a caregiver. This is a come-and-go event.

events.title: Scholastic Book Fair
events.description: The Scholastic Book Fair is returning to Round Rock Public Library July 6-9! Bring home great books to keep or share with friends, and help the Friends of Round Rock Public Library raise money to support the Summer Reading Program .
